course_title: Counselling for Health and Capacity Challenges

learning_outcomes.description: Identify issues that are particularly relevant to situations in which there are conditions or changes that challenge people on the basis of their physical and mental health
Identify the factors that affect the intensity of peoples reactions to health and capacity challenges

Recognise and understand how systems impact on a persons' experience of illness, particularly in the area of social determinants of health, access to services, and Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander health.
Explore the various roles of the counsellor and variety of approaches to various health and capacity challenges
Enhance intersectoral co-operation within the care of those exposed to health challenges
Recognise different approaches to counselling for those experiencing health and capacity challenges in various settings
Critically analyse approaches to care and synthesise to provide a strong basis for evidence-based practice
Analyse concepts that affect the lived experience of  health of people in a holistic approach to health

assessments.assessment_title: In-Class Assessment: Using the BPSS model to explore and formulate
assessments.category: Quiz, Role play/ Simulation, Tutorial/ Problem Set
assessments.task_description:
This assessment is designed to evaluate your clinical skills in reflective listening, asking broader and deeper questions that further your understanding of the client experience (using the biopsychosocial-spiritual (BPSS) model as a framework), and in case formulation (understanding the main presenting issues for the client and why they are occurring). 

During the scheduled workshop in Week 5, you will watch a 10-minute video vignette of a client describing their experience with a health condition or capacity challenge. After watching the video, you will have 50 minutes to complete the assessment tasks.

Assessment Tasks:

Reflective Listening Skills:

a. Identify two instances in the video where you could have made a simple reflection. Provide the specific time point in the client vignette and the reflective statements you would have made.

b. Identify two instances where you could have made a complex reflection. Provide the specific time point in the client vignette and the reflective statements you would have made.

Exploratory Questioning within the BPSS Framework:

a. Identify two questions you would ask the client to gain a broader and deeper understanding of their experience, using the BPSS model as a framework.

Case Formulation (BPSS Model Application):

a. Based on the client's story presented in the video, identify two main presenting issues for the client. For each presenting issue, describe the relevant BPSS factors involved and how they may interact or contribute to the issue.

Please note that you will be evaluated based on your ability to demonstrate reflective listening skills, formulate appropriate questions within the BPSS framework, and identify presenting issues while considering the interplay of biological, psychological, social, and spiritual factors. Assessing these skills will be contingent on your ability to provide clear and succinct written responses that express your approach.

This is an invigilated, in-class assessment. You will complete your responses on your own laptop and submit via Blackboard during the scheduled session. The use of generative AI tools (including ChatGPT, Copilot, Gemini and any other large language model or AI assistant) is not permitted for any part of this assessment. You may not have these tools open or running during the session, and your work must be entirely your own.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This is an invigilated, in-class assessment. You will complete your responses on your own laptop and submit via Blackboard (Turnitin) during the scheduled session.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.assessment_title: Clinical Skills Assessment: Motivational Interviewing Skills for Health Behaviour Change
assessments.task_description:
For this assessment, you will conduct and record a 10-minute motivational interviewing session with a client (a colleague or friend) who needs to make a health behaviour change following a newly diagnosed health condition. The condition is to be selected from the list provided.

Assessment Task:

Your task is to demonstrate the following motivational interviewing skills to help shift the person further towards their health behaviour change:

  1. Building a partnership
  2. Navigating ambivalence and cultivating change talk
  3. Responding to sustain talk and discord
  4. Integrating cultural and contextual considerations

You will be evaluated on your ability to effectively apply these skills during the 10-minute recorded session.

You can assume that the introductory part of your session has been covered already (e.g. introductions, consent and confidentiality, risk assessment). You may begin with the sentence: "So [client name], I can see from your referral that your doctor has recommended you make some changes for your health. Tell me a bit more about that."

Please note that the marking rubric will assess your competence in conducting the motivational interviewing session against the specific criteria outlined, evaluating the demonstration of your therapeutic process as well as content. You should not rely on scripted interactions to demonstrate your therapeutic skills.


Note on the use of AI: This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Via Turnitin. Please keep a copy of your submission receipt.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: On-Air: Illness, Identity and Practice (Simulated Podcast)
assessments.task_description:
Task description

In this assessment you take the role of an invited expert guest on a (simulated) podcast about living well with health and capacity challenges. Your episode focuses on one health condition or capacity challenge, chosen from the list provided, and explores how it can reshape a person's sense of self, and what counselling can offer.

You will record a conversation with a host (a colleague or friend). Your host will ask questions of you (drawing on the provided Host Question Bank), and your role is to respond as the expert guest: explaining, translating and applying your knowledge in an accessible, engaging and natural way.

Across the 10-12 minute recorded episode you will be expected to:

  • introduce the condition and its lived experience in plain language;
  • explain how the condition can affect identity and sense of self, drawing on relevant frameworks from the course;
  • describe what counselling can offer, including at least one approach covered in the course; and
  • illustrate how this translates to practice — for example, by responding to a question such as "what would that look like when you're actually working with someone?" with a brief, concrete example of the approach in action.

Your accompanying show notes (one page) should include a short episode summary, the scholarly sources informing your episode (in [APA 7th]), and one resource a listener could turn to for further support.

Both video and show notes will be submitted via Blackboard.

A note on delivery

This task assesses your ability to translate clinical understanding into genuine, accessible conversation. The strongest podcast episodes are real, flowing discussion. Reading or memorising from a prepared script works against this: where a response appears to be read aloud rather than spoken naturally, it will limit the marks available to you. Prepare thoroughly, then speak from notes and engage with your host as you would in a genuine conversation. Position yourselves on camera so that both you and your host are visible throughout.

Note on the use of AI:

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Via Turnitin. Please keep a copy of your submission receipt.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
